candidates.reasoning: The visible vegetation consists of generic deciduous street trees and common roadside grasses. These species are cosmopolitan to temperate East Asia and do not provide a unique botanical signature to distinguish South Korea from neighboring Japan or China.

The vegetation is typical of temperate East Asian urban environments, which is consistent with many regions in China, but provides no diagnostic evidence to isolate it as the primary location.

The image shows right-hand traffic (RHT) with white dashed lane markings and a white solid edge line. The guardrail design and the specific style of the utility poles and overhead wiring are highly characteristic of South Korean road infrastructure.

The utility poles are concrete with a very specific cross-arm and wiring configuration common in South Korea.
